issues.problem: Phase 1 discovers 48 links per category page but 0 pass the is_product_url() filter because the filter REJECTS the site's actual URL format
issues.details: The site_analysis.json states the product URL pattern is '/en-uk/{category-path}/{product-slug}/{product-code}.html' (e.g., 'https://www.calvinklein.co.uk/en-uk/mens-clothing-tshirts/cotton-stretch-crew-neck-t-shirt/K011455747.html'). However, the Cycle 3 scraper's is_product_url() and DISCOVER_PRODUCT_URLS_JS both EXPLICITLY filter OUT URLs containing '/en-uk/' and '.html' — these are the exact characteristics of the real product URLs! The Cycle 3 fix adopted a wrong pattern /{slug}-{12-char-code} (no /en-uk/ prefix, no .html suffix, uppercase alphanumeric code). The actual 48 links found per category page are likely the correct product URLs matching the site's real format, but they are all discarded by the filter. This is the same root issue as Cycle 2 but now the filter is explicitly excluding the correct URLs.
issues.suggested_fix:
FIX is_product_url() and DISCOVER_PRODUCT_URLS_JS to MATCH the site's actual URL format from site_analysis.json:

1. The real pattern is: /en-uk/{category}/{product-slug}/K{digits}.html
2. Example: /en-uk/mens-clothing-tshirts/cotton-stretch-crew-neck-t-shirt/K011455747.html

In is_product_url(): Remove the lines that skip URLs with '/en-uk/' and '.html'. Instead, check for the K-code pattern: path should match /en-uk/.+/K\d{5,}\.html$

In DISCOVER_PRODUCT_URLS_JS: Remove the lines 'if (path.indexOf("/en-uk/") !== -1) continue;' and 'if (path.indexOf(".html") !== -1) continue;'. Change the regex match to look for /en-uk/.../K{digits}.html pattern.

Specific code changes:
- is_product_url(): Change to match '/en-uk/' prefix AND '.html' suffix AND contain '/K' followed by digits
- DISCOVER_PRODUCT_URLS_JS: Remove the two 'continue' lines that skip /en-uk/ and .html URLs

issues.problem: SEED_CATEGORY_URLS still contains 'https://www.calvinklein-co-uk/womens-bestsellers' (hyphen in domain instead of dot)
issues.details: One seed URL uses 'calvinklein-co-uk' (hyphens) instead of 'calvinklein.co.uk' (dots). This was flagged in Cycle 1 and Cycle 2 but not fixed. The scraper navigates to it but likely gets no product links from that URL.

script_checks.error_message: Phase 1 discovered 0 product URLs from 8 seed category pages. 48 links found per page (for 6/8 seeds) but 0 passed the is_product_url() filter. The filter explicitly rejects URLs with '/en-uk/' prefix and '.html' suffix — which is the site's actual product URL format. Scraper exited with code 1: 'No product URLs found — cannot proceed'.

previous_cycle_fixes_assessment.cycle_2_issue_product_discovery: NOT FIXED - Made WORSE. The Cycle 3 fix changed the regex to match /{slug}-{12-char-code} (no /en-uk/, no .html) but the site's actual format IS /en-uk/{category}/{slug}/K{code}.html. The fix explicitly excludes the correct URLs.
previous_cycle_fixes_assessment.cycle_2_issue_proxy_format: RESOLVED - Default is now --no-proxy (no-proxy is default True), so proxy string format issue no longer triggers.
previous_cycle_fixes_assessment.cycle_2_issue_seed_url_typo: NOT FIXED - Still uses calvinklein-co-uk with hyphens for one seed URL.
previous_cycle_fixes_assessment.cycle_1_issue_spa_navigation: FIXED - driver.get() used for product pages.

feedback_for_writer.summary: Cycle 3 retest: The sole remaining blocker from Cycle 2 (product discovery filter) was attempted but fixed in the WRONG DIRECTION. The Cycle 3 changes adopted a URL pattern (/{slug}-{12-char-code}) that is the OPPOSITE of the site's actual format (/en-uk/{category}/{slug}/K{code}.html). The site_analysis.json clearly documents the real URL pattern. The 48 links found per category page are likely the correct product URLs, but the filter explicitly rejects them. Fix the is_product_url() function and DISCOVER_PRODUCT_URLS_JS to match the actual SFCC URL format documented in site_analysis.json.

product_discovery.issue: is_product_url() and DISCOVER_PRODUCT_URLS_JS reject URLs with /en-uk/ prefix and .html suffix — which is EXACTLY the site's real product URL format. The 48 links per page ARE the real product links but get discarded.
product_discovery.fix:
Rewrite is_product_url() to match the actual pattern from site_analysis.json: path contains '/en-uk/', ends with '.html', and has a K-code (K followed by digits) in the path. E.g.: /en-uk/mens-clothing-tshirts/cotton-stretch-crew-neck-t-shirt/K011455747.html. Specifically:

1. In is_product_url(): Remove 'if path.endswith(".html"): return False' and 'if "/en-uk/" in path: return False'. Instead add: match = re.search(r'/en-uk/.+/K\d{5,}\.html$', path). Add this check and return True if matched.

2. In DISCOVER_PRODUCT_URLS_JS: Remove the two continue statements for /en-uk/ and .html. Change the regex from /\/[a-z0-9-]+-[A-Z0-9]{10,}$/ to something that matches /en-uk/.../K...html pattern.

3. The _PRODUCT_URL_RE regex at module level also needs updating.

Note: The sample URL from site_analysis is: https://www.calvinklein.co.uk/en-uk/mens-clothing-tshirts/cotton-stretch-crew-neck-t-shirt/K011455747.html

seed_url_typo.issue: One seed URL still uses calvinklein-co-uk (hyphens) instead of calvinklein.co.uk
seed_url_typo.fix: Correct the domain in SEED_CATEGORY_URLS from calvinklein-co-uk to calvinklein.co.uk
